<<<KOT:equalitarianism>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Equalitarianism is a normative political-philosophical doctrine centred on abstract principles (social equality, moral status, rights, treatment under law) that require grounding in mature concepts of society, justice, and political obligation. These are not expressible in the prime lexicon or as molecules over the current catalog: the concept belongs to political theory, not the everyday perceptual/bodily tier."}
<<<END:equalitarianism>>>>

<<<KOT:falconine>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Falconine is a relational adjective anchored to the natural-kind concept falcon (or hawk), which is not in the molecule catalog. Although a falcon is a concrete kind, its identity conditions belong to zoology. Without a referenceable molecule for falcon or hawk, the resemblance-relation cannot be grounded in the allowed lexicon."}
<<<END:falconine>>>>

<<<KOT:filaggrin>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Filaggrin is a biochemical protein defined by its molecular structure (post-translational processing from profilaggrin, binding to keratin fibres, location in epithelial cells, genetic locus FLG on chromosome 1q21). Its identity conditions are entirely molecular-biological and cannot be expressed in the prime lexicon or as a molecule anchored to everyday percepts and bodily experience."}
<<<END:filaggrin>>>>
